assignments.title: AVL & Red-Black Tree Complex Benchmark Implementation
assignments.details: Implement complete tree rotations, insertion, deletion, and analyze clock-time performance relative to tree depth. Submit zipped Java project.

assignments.title: Normal Form Normalization Clinic System Case Study
assignments.details: Decompose a flat clinic registration table into 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF structures. Include multi-valued dependencies.

projects.title: Multi-threaded Enterprise Socket Server and Client
projects.details: Build a socket chat server capable of handling concurrent clients, maintaining logs, and utilizing thread-pools. Must demonstrate clean OOP design patterns.

studyPlans.focusArea: Review Data Structures: Heap Tree and AVL Rotations
Practice Boyce-Codd Normal Form decomposition rules
Solve discrete proofs and proposition matrices

reminders.title: Revise Discrete Mathematics structural induction proofs
Prepare DBMS team normal form presentation outline

gemmaActivities.message: Class Timetable PDF analyzed and structured.
gemmaActivities.reasoning: I analyzed the file 'Class_Timetable_v1.pdf'. Determined that the curriculum matches 'Computer Science Year 2 Semester 1' of State University of Technology. Extracted 5 distinct lecture schedules: CSC 211, CSC 212, CSC 213, CSC 214, SMA 201. Assigned correct classrooms in Tech Block and Science Complex. Established Lecture Hall B as the primary lecture location.

gemmaActivities.message: Exam Timetable PDF processed.
gemmaActivities.reasoning: Extracted final exam timetable from 'Exam_Timetable_v1.pdf'. Identified 3 exams commencing Dec 8, Dec 10, and Dec 12. Marked CSC 211 and SMA 201 as high-density exams scheduled in the Graduation Pavilion. Assessed spacing: Only 1 day of gap exists between CSC 211 and CSC 213. Triggered early-alert planning sequence.

gemmaActivities.message: Autonomous study schedule generated.
gemmaActivities.reasoning: I assessed the upcoming deadlines and exam density. Formulated a 5-day balanced study plan. Prescribed 10 hours of active weekly study. Allocated Monday evening to CSC 211 (AVL & Red-Black tree complexity) because it is a critical skill gap, and Tuesday evening to CSC 212 multi-threaded logic.

scholarships.title: Google Generation Scholarship (EMEA)
scholarships.eligibility: Computer Science undergraduate students, Year 2-3, demonstrating academic excellence and passion for technology.

scholarships.title: DeepMind Scholarship for Undergraduate Students
scholarships.eligibility: Undergraduate STEM major students in selected universities, including State University of Technology. Passionate about AI research.

scholarships.title: National STEM Excellence Grant
scholarships.eligibility: Computer Science majors at State University of Technology with GPA > 3.7

scholarships.title: Higher Education Loans Board Merit Scheme
scholarships.eligibility: Undergraduate students from needy backgrounds enrolled in accredited universities including State University of Technology.

scholarships.title: ALX Software Engineering Mentorship & Cohort
scholarships.eligibility: Tech enthusiasts eager to master front-end and full-stack engineering alongside industry veterans.

scholarships.title: Women in Tech Mentorship Program
scholarships.eligibility: Female STEM undergraduates in regional universities wanting training in Network Engineering and Cloud Computing.

campusEvents.title: University Tech Hackathon 2026
campusEvents.description: Annual hackathon for local developers and university students. Track topics: AI in Agriculture, Mobile Web, FinTech. Organized by GDSC.

campusEvents.title: Google Developer Student Club (GDSC) Onboarding
campusEvents.description: Meet the core team, learn about technology study jams, and hear about global solutions challenge guidelines. Refreshments provided.

campusEvents.title: AI and Machine Learning in Agriculture Seminar
campusEvents.description: Special workshop with guest researchers demonstrating crop yield detection using computer vision and lightweight Edge-ML models.
